findings.title: Admin route without authorization check
findings.description: backend\src\routes\contact.js appears to define admin-related routes but does not use authorization middleware. Any authenticated user could access admin functionality.
findings.suggestion: Add authorize("admin") middleware to all admin routes.

findings.title: Potential IDOR vulnerability — missing ownership check
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\allotmentSettingsController.js accesses resources by ID from request params but may not verify the requesting user owns the resource. This is an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability.
findings.suggestion: Add ownership verification: ensure req.user.id or req.user.hotelId matches the resource.

findings.title: Potential IDOR vulnerability — missing ownership check

findings.title: Potential NoSQL injection vulnerability
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\corporateCreditController.js appears to pass user input directly into MongoDB queries. Attackers can inject operators like $gt, $ne, $regex to bypass query logic.
findings.suggestion: Use express-mongo-sanitize middleware and validate/sanitize all query parameters.

findings.title: N+1 query pattern detected
findings.description: Database query inside a loop at line 617 in backend\src\controllers\addOnController.js. Each iteration triggers a separate DB query, causing O(N) queries instead of O(1). For 100 items, this means 100 DB round-trips.
findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.title: 1 unbounded .find() queries without .limit()
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\addOnController.js has 1 .find() calls without .limit(). These will return all matching documents, which can be catastrophic with large collections (millions of docs).
findings.suggestion: Add .limit() to all .find() queries. Implement cursor-based or offset pagination.

findings.title: N+1 query pattern detected
findings.description: Database query inside a loop at line 636 in backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js. Each iteration triggers a separate DB query, causing O(N) queries instead of O(1). For 100 items, this means 100 DB round-trips.
findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.title: 2 unbounded .find() queries without .limit()
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js has 2 .find() calls without .limit(). These will return all matching documents, which can be catastrophic with large collections (millions of docs).
findings.suggestion: Add .limit() to all .find() queries. Implement cursor-based or offset pagination.

findings.title: Consider using .lean() for read-only queries
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js has 9 queries that may benefit from .lean(). Lean queries return plain objects instead of Mongoose documents, using ~5x less memory and being significantly faster.
findings.suggestion: Add .lean() to queries where you only read data (no .save()/.validate() needed).

findings.title: Synchronous file operations block the event loop
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js uses 1 synchronous file system operations (mkdirSync). These block the Node.js event loop and prevent concurrent request handling.
findings.suggestion: Use async variants (readFile, writeFile, readdir) from fs/promises.

findings.suggestion: Cache results in Redis with a reasonable TTL (e.g., 5-15 minutes).

findings.title: Synchronous file operations block the event loop

findings.suggestion: Use async variants (readFile, writeFile, readdir) from fs/promises.

findings.title: Read-modify-write race condition
findings.description:
At line 485 in backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js: A document is read, modified in JavaScript, and saved back without atomic operations or a transaction. If two requests execute concurrently, one update will be lost.

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Consider using atomic operators or transactions for this operation.

findings.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
findings.description:
At line 794 in backend\src\controllers\allotmentController.js: Availability is checked and then a booking is created in separate operations without locking. Two concurrent requests for the same room/time can both pass the availability check and both create bookings (TOCTOU race condition).

This is the #1 concurrency bug in booking systems.
findings.suggestion:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

findings.title: Multiple writes without transaction in critical operation
findings.description: At line 1001 in backend\src\controllers\bookingEngineController.js: Multiple database write operations are performed without a MongoDB transaction. If any operation fails, the database will be left in an inconsistent state (partial update).
findings.suggestion: Wrap related write operations in a MongoDB session/transaction: const session = await mongoose.startSession(); await session.withTransaction(async () => { ... });

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Consider using atomic operators or transactions for this operation.

findings.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
findings.description:
At line 206 in backend\src\controllers\dayUseController.js: Availability is checked and then a booking is created in separate operations without locking. Two concurrent requests for the same room/time can both pass the availability check and both create bookings (TOCTOU race condition).

This is the #1 concurrency bug in booking systems.
findings.suggestion:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

findings.title: Date comparisons without timezone handling
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js compares dates (check-in/check-out) without explicit timezone handling. Date comparisons across timezones can cause off-by-one-day errors, leading to bookings on wrong dates.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all dates to UTC at the API boundary. Store dates in UTC. Convert to local timezone only for display.

findings.title: Missing check-in/check-out date validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js handles check-in and check-out dates but may not validate that check-out is after check-in. This can create negative-duration bookings.
findings.suggestion: Add validation: if (checkOutDate <= checkInDate) throw new Error("Check-out must be after check-in");

findings.title: Date comparisons without timezone handling
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\bookingEngineController.js compares dates (check-in/check-out) without explicit timezone handling. Date comparisons across timezones can cause off-by-one-day errors, leading to bookings on wrong dates.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all dates to UTC at the API boundary. Store dates in UTC. Convert to local timezone only for display.

findings.suggestion: Add validation: if (checkOutDate <= checkInDate) throw new Error("Check-out must be after check-in");

findings.title: Missing booking status transition validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\bookingFormController.js updates booking status but doesn't validate the transition. This allows invalid transitions like cancelled → checked_in or checked_out → pending, corrupting the booking state machine.
findings.suggestion: Implement a state machine or transition map that validates: only allowed current_status → new_status transitions should proceed.

findings.title: Booking cancellation without refund processing
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\corporateTrackingController.js handles booking cancellation but doesn't appear to process refunds. Guests who paid for cancelled bookings should receive appropriate refunds based on the cancellation policy.
findings.suggestion: Implement cancellation refund logic: check cancellation policy, calculate refund amount, initiate refund via payment provider.

findings.suggestion: Add validation: if (checkOutDate <= checkInDate) throw new Error("Check-out must be after check-in");

findings.title: Date comparisons without timezone handling
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\multiCurrencyRateController.js compares dates (check-in/check-out) without explicit timezone handling. Date comparisons across timezones can cause off-by-one-day errors, leading to bookings on wrong dates.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all dates to UTC at the API boundary. Store dates in UTC. Convert to local timezone only for display.

findings.title: Date comparisons without timezone handling
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\rateManagementController.js compares dates (check-in/check-out) without explicit timezone handling. Date comparisons across timezones can cause off-by-one-day errors, leading to bookings on wrong dates.

findings.title: [CONSOLIDATED] "Missing check-in/check-out date validation" — additional occurrences suppressed

findings.title: Booking cancellation without refund processing
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomChargeController.js handles booking cancellation but doesn't appear to process refunds. Guests who paid for cancelled bookings should receive appropriate refunds based on the cancellation policy.
findings.suggestion: Implement cancellation refund logic: check cancellation policy, calculate refund amount, initiate refund via payment provider.

findings.title: Cancellation may not release room inventory
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomChargeController.js cancels bookings but may not release the room back to available inventory. This causes phantom bookings that block rooms from being re-sold.
findings.suggestion: When cancelling a booking, always update room availability and inventory for the cancelled date range.

findings.title: Tax may be calculated after total
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomTaxController.js: Tax appears to be calculated after the total is set. This could mean tax is not included in the final total presented to the guest.
findings.suggestion: Calculate all taxes before computing the final total. total = subtotal + taxes + serviceCharges.

findings.title: No-show handling without penalty charge
findings.description: backend\src\services\bookingChannelService.js handles no-shows but doesn't appear to charge a penalty. Most hotels charge a no-show fee (typically one night's stay).
findings.suggestion: Implement no-show penalty charging based on hotel policy.

findings.title: Missing booking status transition validation
findings.description: backend\src\services\bookingComConnector.js updates booking status but doesn't validate the transition. This allows invalid transitions like cancelled → checked_in or checked_out → pending, corrupting the booking state machine.
findings.suggestion: Implement a state machine or transition map that validates: only allowed current_status → new_status transitions should proceed.

findings.title: Room assignment without availability check
findings.description: At line 550 in backend\src\routes\rooms.js: A room is assigned from user input without checking if it's available. This can assign occupied, blocked, or out-of-order rooms to guests.
findings.suggestion: Always verify room availability before assignment: check status, existing bookings for the date range, and maintenance blocks.

findings.suggestion: Implement overbooking management: waitlist, guest relocation, compensation offers, and alerts for front desk.

findings.title: Booking model may be missing essential fields: checkIn, checkOut, totalAmount, paymentStatus
findings.description: The Booking model may not have all essential fields. Missing: checkIn, checkOut, totalAmount, paymentStatus. These fields are critical for a functional PMS booking system.
findings.suggestion: Add missing fields to ensure complete booking data capture.

findings.title: Payment settlement without reconciliation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\billingSessionController.js handles settlements but may not reconcile with the payment provider. Discrepancies between internal records and Stripe can go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\financialController.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\generalLedgerController.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.title: Stripe webhook may not receive raw body
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js handles Stripe webhooks but may not be configured to receive the raw request body. Stripe signature verification requires the raw body, but Express JSON middleware parses it.
findings.suggestion: Use express.raw({ type: "application/json" }) middleware for the webhook route, before any JSON body parser.

findings.title: Refund without amount validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js processes refunds but may not validate the refund amount against the original payment. This could allow refunding more than what was paid.
findings.suggestion: Validate: refundAmount <= originalPaymentAmount - previousRefunds. Track cumulative refunds per payment.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment creation without idempotency key
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomChargeController.js creates payments without an idempotency key. Network retries or user double-clicks can create duplicate charges.
findings.suggestion: Pass idempotencyKey to Stripe API calls: stripe.paymentIntents.create({ ... }, { idempotencyKey: req.headers["idempotency-key"] })

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\services\otaPayloadService.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment system may not support partial payments
findings.description: backend\src\services\payloadRetentionService.js may not handle partial/split payments. Hotels commonly receive partial payments (advance, balance at checkout) and split payments (cash + card).
findings.suggestion: Support partial payments: track totalAmount, paidAmount, and balanceDue. Allow multiple payment records per booking.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\models\Invoice.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: [CONSOLIDATED] "Financial ledger without balance verification" — additional occurrences suppressed
findings.description: This finding type appeared more than 10 times. First 10 instances are listed individually above. Remaining instances are consolidated here to reduce noise. Address the pattern systemically rather than fixing each instance individually.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\models\OTAPayload.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.title: Payment system may not support partial payments
findings.description: backend\src\models\OTAPayload.js may not handle partial/split payments. Hotels commonly receive partial payments (advance, balance at checkout) and split payments (cash + card).
findings.suggestion: Support partial payments: track totalAmount, paidAmount, and balanceDue. Allow multiple payment records per booking.

findings.title: Refund without amount validation
findings.description: backend\src\models\Payment.js processes refunds but may not validate the refund amount against the original payment. This could allow refunding more than what was paid.
findings.suggestion: Validate: refundAmount <= originalPaymentAmount - previousRefunds. Track cumulative refunds per payment.

findings.title: Payment creation without idempotency key
findings.description: backend\src\models\Payment.js creates payments without an idempotency key. Network retries or user double-clicks can create duplicate charges.
findings.suggestion: Pass idempotencyKey to Stripe API calls: stripe.paymentIntents.create({ ... }, { idempotencyKey: req.headers["idempotency-key"] })

findings.title: Stripe webhook may not receive raw body
findings.description: backend\src\models\PaymentMethod.js handles Stripe webhooks but may not be configured to receive the raw request body. Stripe signature verification requires the raw body, but Express JSON middleware parses it.
findings.suggestion: Use express.raw({ type: "application/json" }) middleware for the webhook route, before any JSON body parser.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\models\PaymentMethod.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Payment system may not support partial payments
findings.description: backend\src\models\PaymentReconciliation.js may not handle partial/split payments. Hotels commonly receive partial payments (advance, balance at checkout) and split payments (cash + card).
findings.suggestion: Support partial payments: track totalAmount, paidAmount, and balanceDue. Allow multiple payment records per booking.

findings.title: Bulk deleteMany without tenant scope
findings.description: At line 646 in backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js: Bulk operation "deleteMany" does not scope by hotelId. This affects ALL tenants' data — a deleteMany without hotelId could wipe records across all hotels.
findings.suggestion: Always include hotelId in bulk operations: .deleteMany({ hotelId, ...conditions })

findings.title: Aggregation pipeline without tenant $match
findings.description: At line 114 in backend\src\controllers\adminTravelDashboardController.js: Aggregation pipeline does not start with a $match on hotelId. This computes analytics/reports across ALL hotels' data, leaking business intelligence between tenants.
findings.suggestion: Always start aggregation with: { $match: { hotelId } } as the first stage.

findings.title: Client-controlled hotelId without verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminTravelDashboardController.js reads hotelId from client request (body/query/params) without verifying the user has access to that property. A user at Hotel A could pass Hotel B's ID to access their data.
findings.suggestion: Always verify hotelId against req.user.hotelId or user's allowed properties. Never trust client-provided hotelId.

findings.title: Client-controlled hotelId without verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js reads hotelId from client request (body/query/params) without verifying the user has access to that property. A user at Hotel A could pass Hotel B's ID to access their data.
findings.suggestion: Always verify hotelId against req.user.hotelId or user's allowed properties. Never trust client-provided hotelId.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: Booking query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 535 in backend\src\controllers\analyticsController.js: A query on "Booking" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's booking records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: Booking.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Aggregation pipeline without tenant $match
findings.description: At line 581 in backend\src\controllers\analyticsController.js: Aggregation pipeline does not start with a $match on hotelId. This computes analytics/reports across ALL hotels' data, leaking business intelligence between tenants.
findings.suggestion: Always start aggregation with: { $match: { hotelId } } as the first stage.

findings.title: Housekeeping workflow: 1 capabilities missing
findings.description:
Housekeeping workflow gaps:
  - real-time status updates for front desk

Housekeeping is THE most operationally critical department. Without proper workflow automation, rooms aren't cleaned on time, VIP rooms aren't prioritized, and front desk doesn't know which rooms are ready.
findings.suggestion: Implement complete housekeeping lifecycle: checkout triggers dirty → assignment → cleaning → inspection → clean. Real-time sync to front desk.

findings.title: Guest laundry service not tracked
findings.description: Guest laundry requests and billing may not be tracked. This is a revenue stream for hotels — express laundry charges, dry cleaning, etc.
findings.suggestion: Add: guest laundry request form → pickup scheduling → item tracking → billing → delivery confirmation.

findings.title: Missing audit trail for deletion in addOnController.js
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\addOnController.js performs "deletion" operations without logging to an audit trail. For compliance and forensics, all critical operations must be logged with who, what, when, and from where.
findings.suggestion: Log to AuditLog: { userId, action, resource, resourceId, changes, ipAddress, timestamp }.

findings.suggestion: Log to AuditLog: { userId, action, resource, resourceId, changes, ipAddress, timestamp }.

findings.title: Missing consent capture during user/guest creation
findings.description: backend\src\services\analytics\GuestSegmentationService.js creates users/guests without capturing GDPR consent. GDPR Article 6 requires a lawful basis for processing personal data — explicit consent is the most common basis for hotels.
findings.suggestion: Add consent fields: { gdprConsent: Boolean, consentDate: Date, marketingConsent: Boolean }. Record what was consented to.

findings.title: [CONSOLIDATED] "Missing consent capture during user/guest creation" — additional occurrences suppressed
findings.description: This finding type appeared more than 10 times. First 10 instances are listed individually above. Remaining instances are consolidated here to reduce noise. Address the pattern systemically rather than fixing each instance individually.
findings.suggestion: Add consent fields: { gdprConsent: Boolean, consentDate: Date, marketingConsent: Boolean }. Record what was consented to.

findings.title: GDPR right-to-deletion may be incomplete
findings.description: backend\src\routes\meetUpRequests.js handles data deletion but may not cascade to related collections (bookings, payments, reviews, communications). GDPR Article 17 requires erasure of ALL personal data.
findings.suggestion: Implement cascading deletion: delete or anonymize user data across ALL collections (bookings, payments, reviews, notifications, audit logs). Consider anonymization over hard-delete for financial records.

findings.title: GDPR right-to-deletion may be incomplete
findings.description: backend\src\routes\rolePermissions.js handles data deletion but may not cascade to related collections (bookings, payments, reviews, communications). GDPR Article 17 requires erasure of ALL personal data.
findings.suggestion: Implement cascading deletion: delete or anonymize user data across ALL collections (bookings, payments, reviews, notifications, audit logs). Consider anonymization over hard-delete for financial records.

findings.title: No data retention policy: AuditLog.js
findings.description: backend\src\models\AuditLog.js stores temporal data (logs, sessions, metrics) without TTL/expiration. Data grows indefinitely, violating GDPR's data minimization principle and consuming storage.
findings.suggestion: Add MongoDB TTL index: schema.index({ createdAt: 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 90 * 24 * 60 * 60 }) for 90-day retention.

findings.title: PCI-DSS: Raw card data handled server-side
findings.description: backend\src\models\CorporateCompany.js handles raw card data (numbers, CVV, expiry). PCI-DSS prohibits storing CVV/CVC after authorization. Server-side card handling requires PCI-DSS Level 1 certification (costly).
findings.suggestion: Use Stripe.js/Elements for client-side tokenization. Server should only handle Stripe tokens/payment method IDs, never raw card data.

findings.title: User/Guest model lacks GDPR consent fields
findings.description: The User/Guest model does not have GDPR consent tracking fields. Without these, there's no way to prove consent was given, when, or if the user requested deletion.
findings.suggestion: Add: gdprConsent (Boolean), consentDate (Date), marketingConsent (Boolean), deletedAt (Date for soft-delete), anonymized (Boolean).

findings.title: 213/214 error responses lack structured format
findings.description: Many error responses don't include both an error code and message. Without structured errors, frontend can't show meaningful messages to users or implement error-specific handling (retry vs. show error vs. redirect).
findings.suggestion: Standardize errors: { success: false, error: { code: "BOOKING_CONFLICT", message: "Room already booked for these dates", details: [...] } }

findings.title: ~115 list queries without pagination
findings.description: Approximately 115 database queries return full result sets without pagination. This causes: (1) slow response times with growing data, (2) high memory usage, (3) poor UX as the system scales. A hotel with 10,000+ bookings will choke on unpaginated list endpoints.
findings.suggestion: Implement cursor-based or offset pagination on ALL list endpoints. Return: { data: [...], pagination: { page, pageSize, total, totalPages } }.

findings.title: POST used for read operations in analytics.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\analytics.js uses POST for read operations (search/list/get). POST requests aren't cacheable, can't be bookmarked, and violate REST semantics. This hurts CDN caching and browser behavior.
findings.suggestion: Use GET with query params for reads. For complex search filters, GET with JSON-encoded query param or POST /search is acceptable.

findings.title: DELETE endpoint without resource ID in userPreferences.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\userPreferences.js has a DELETE route on the collection root (no :id). This could accidentally delete all resources.
findings.suggestion: DELETE should always target a specific resource: DELETE /resource/:id. For bulk delete, use POST /resource/batch-delete with IDs in the body.

findings.title: Inconsistent URL naming conventions (camelCase: 399, kebab-case: 476)
findings.description: API routes mix naming conventions: camelCase: 399, kebab-case: 476. Inconsistency makes the API harder to learn and use. REST convention recommends kebab-case for URLs.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on kebab-case for URLs: /room-types, /booking-engine, /daily-routine-check.

findings.title: CRITICAL MODULE UNTESTED: payment
findings.description: No test files found for "payment". Why this matters: Payment bugs = financial loss + legal liability. Without tests, any change to this module risks breaking production with no safety net.
findings.suggestion: Create payment.test.js with: unit tests for core logic, integration tests with DB, edge case tests, and error handling tests.

findings.title: CRITICAL MODULE UNTESTED: checkout
findings.description: No test files found for "checkout". Why this matters: Checkout errors = billing disputes. Without tests, any change to this module risks breaking production with no safety net.
findings.suggestion: Create checkout.test.js with: unit tests for core logic, integration tests with DB, edge case tests, and error handling tests.

findings.suggestion: Create roomAvailability.test.js with: unit tests for core logic, integration tests with DB, edge case tests, and error handling tests.

findings.suggestion: Create settlement.test.js with: unit tests for core logic, integration tests with DB, edge case tests, and error handling tests.

findings.title: CRITICAL MODULE UNTESTED: channelManager
findings.description: No test files found for "channelManager". Why this matters: OTA sync bugs = rate parity violations + booking conflicts. Without tests, any change to this module risks breaking production with no safety net.
findings.suggestion: Create channelManager.test.js with: unit tests for core logic, integration tests with DB, edge case tests, and error handling tests.

findings.title: 108/108 controllers have no tests (0% coverage)
findings.description: 108 out of 108 controllers have no corresponding test file. Controllers handle all API request processing — untested controllers mean untested API endpoints.
findings.suggestion: Prioritize testing controllers for: auth, bookings, payments, room management, billing, and guest management.

findings.title: 168/170 services have no tests (1% coverage)
findings.description: 168 out of 170 services have no corresponding test file. Services contain all business logic — untested services mean untested business rules.
findings.suggestion: Prioritize testing services for: booking workflow, payment processing, rate calculation, availability, tax computation.

findings.title: Critical models without validation tests: Payment, Settlement
findings.description: Models Payment, Settlement have no test coverage. Model validation (required fields, enums, min/max, custom validators) runs on every save — bugs here corrupt the database silently.
findings.suggestion: Test: required field enforcement, enum validation, custom validators, pre-save hooks, virtual fields, and index uniqueness.

findings.title: Critical middleware untested: rateLimit
findings.description: Middleware [rateLimit] has no tests. Auth middleware bugs = security breach. Validation middleware bugs = data corruption. Error handler bugs = leaked stack traces.
findings.suggestion: Test: valid/invalid tokens, expired tokens, missing headers, role enforcement, malformed input handling, error serialization.

findings.title: 7 critical user flows have no E2E tests
findings.description: No end-to-end tests for: check-in process, check-out process, payment processing, guest management, cancellation + refund, staff operations, reports + analytics. E2E tests validate the complete user journey. Without them, integration issues between frontend and backend go undetected until production.
findings.suggestion: Add Playwright E2E tests for each critical flow. Priority: booking creation → payment → check-in → check-out.

findings.title: Frontend test coverage extremely low: ~2.1% (13/609)
findings.description: Only 13 test files for ~609 frontend components/pages. Critical UI bugs (broken forms, inaccessible elements, state bugs) ship without detection.
findings.suggestion: Prioritize testing: BookingForm, PaymentForm, TapeChart, Dashboard, AuthForms. Use React Testing Library for component tests.

findings.title: Very low test-to-code ratio: 0.06 (39 tests / 628 source files)
findings.description: The test-to-code ratio is 0.06. Industry standard for production systems is 1:1 or higher. With only 39 test files for 628 source files, most code changes are untestable.
findings.suggestion: Target 1:1 test-to-code ratio for critical modules. Start with the highest-risk areas: payments, bookings, auth.

findings.title: Accessibility: 4 form inputs without labels in AdminAddOnServices.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminAddOnServices.tsx has form inputs without associated labels. Screen readers cannot identify these inputs, making the form unusable for visually impaired users. This also violates WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 1.3.1.
findings.suggestion: Add <label htmlFor="inputId"> or aria-label attribute to every form input.

findings.title: Memory leak: useEffect without cleanup in AdminAddOnServices.tsx
findings.description: At line 97: useEffect subscribes to events/timers but has no cleanup function. On component unmount, the subscription stays active, causing memory leaks and "setState on unmounted component" warnings.
findings.suggestion: Return a cleanup function from useEffect: return () => { clearInterval(timer); unsubscribe(); }.

findings.title: Page without error boundary: AdminAPIManagement.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminAPIManagement.tsx has no error boundary. If any child component throws, the ENTIRE page crashes with a white screen. Users see nothing — no error message, no way to recover.
findings.suggestion: Wrap page content in <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorPage />}>. Import from your ErrorBoundary component.

findings.title: Page without error boundary: AdminAutomation.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminAutomation.tsx has no error boundary. If any child component throws, the ENTIRE page crashes with a white screen. Users see nothing — no error message, no way to recover.
findings.suggestion: Wrap page content in <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorPage />}>. Import from your ErrorBoundary component.

findings.title: Excessive TypeScript 'any' usage: 7 in AdminBillMessages.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminBillMessages.tsx uses 'any' type 7 times. Each 'any' disables type checking, defeating TypeScript's purpose. Type errors that TypeScript would catch become runtime bugs.
findings.suggestion: Replace any with proper types. Use unknown for truly unknown types, then narrow with type guards.

findings.title: Form without client-side validation in AdminBillMessages.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminBillMessages.tsx has a form without apparent validation. Users can submit invalid data, causing backend errors with poor error messages. Client-side validation provides instant feedback.
findings.suggestion: Use react-hook-form with Zod schema validation. Validate on blur for individual fields, on submit for the full form.

findings.title: Memory leak: useEffect without cleanup in AdminBlacklist.tsx
findings.description: At line 103: useEffect subscribes to events/timers but has no cleanup function. On component unmount, the subscription stays active, causing memory leaks and "setState on unmounted component" warnings.
findings.suggestion: Return a cleanup function from useEffect: return () => { clearInterval(timer); unsubscribe(); }.

findings.title: 8 console statements in AdminBookingFormBuilder.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminBookingFormBuilder.tsx has 8 console statements. These pollute the browser console in production, can leak sensitive data, and slow down rendering.
findings.suggestion: Remove console.log statements or use a logger that disables in production. Keep console.error for genuine error reporting.

findings.title: Accessibility: Clickable div without keyboard support in AdminBookings.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminBookings.tsx has clickable <div> elements without keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot interact with these elements. Use <button> instead, or add role="button", tabIndex, and onKeyDown.
findings.suggestion: Replace <div onClick> with <button onClick>. If div is required, add: role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}.

findings.title: Memory leak: useEffect without cleanup in AdminDailyCheckManagement.tsx
findings.description: At line 80: useEffect subscribes to events/timers but has no cleanup function. On component unmount, the subscription stays active, causing memory leaks and "setState on unmounted component" warnings.
findings.suggestion: Return a cleanup function from useEffect: return () => { clearInterval(timer); unsubscribe(); }.

findings.title: 3 API calls without error handling in AdminDailyCheckManagement.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminDailyCheckManagement.tsx makes 3 API calls without error handling. Network failures, 500 errors, or auth expiry cause uncaught promise rejections. Users see nothing — the action silently fails.
findings.suggestion: Handle API errors: show toast notification, display inline error message, or redirect to error page. Use React Query's onError callback.

findings.title: 6 console statements in AdminDailyCheckManagement.tsx
findings.description: frontend\src\pages\admin\AdminDailyCheckManagement.tsx has 6 console statements. These pollute the browser console in production, can leak sensitive data, and slow down rendering.
findings.suggestion: Remove console.log statements or use a logger that disables in production. Keep console.error for genuine error reporting.

findings.title: No degradation strategy for Email (SMTP) in bookingEngineService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\bookingEngineService.js calls Email (SMTP) without timeout, retry limit, or circuit breaker. If Email (SMTP) is slow or down: Notifications silently lost. Without a timeout, requests hang indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool.
findings.suggestion: Add: (1) Timeout: AbortController with 30s limit. (2) Retry with exponential backoff (max 3). (3) Circuit breaker: after 5 failures, skip for 30s. (4) Fallback: queue for later processing.

findings.title: Email sent synchronously without queue in bookingEngineService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\bookingEngineService.js sends emails synchronously in the request path. If the SMTP server is slow/down: (1) the API response is delayed, (2) the email is lost on failure. Critical emails (booking confirmations, payment receipts) must be reliable.
findings.suggestion: Queue emails through Bull/Redis queue. Process asynchronously with retry logic. Store email status for monitoring.

findings.title: Critical service without retry logic: bookingEngineService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\bookingEngineService.js handles critical operations without retry logic. Transient failures (network blip, temporary DB lock) cause permanent failure instead of recovering.
findings.suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures: retry 3 times with 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Only retry idempotent operations.

findings.title: Critical service without retry logic: bookingFormService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\bookingFormService.js handles critical operations without retry logic. Transient failures (network blip, temporary DB lock) cause permanent failure instead of recovering.
findings.suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures: retry 3 times with 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Only retry idempotent operations.

findings.description: backend\src\services\cancellationRefundService.js calls Stripe without timeout, retry limit, or circuit breaker. If Stripe is slow or down: Payments fail entirely. Without a timeout, requests hang indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool.
findings.suggestion: Add: (1) Timeout: AbortController with 30s limit. (2) Retry with exponential backoff (max 3). (3) Circuit breaker: after 5 failures, skip for 30s. (4) Fallback: queue for later processing.

findings.title: No degradation strategy for OTA/Booking.com in overbookingManager.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\overbookingManager.js calls OTA/Booking.com without timeout, retry limit, or circuit breaker. If OTA/Booking.com is slow or down: Channel sync breaks. Without a timeout, requests hang indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool.
findings.suggestion: Add: (1) Timeout: AbortController with 30s limit. (2) Retry with exponential backoff (max 3). (3) Circuit breaker: after 5 failures, skip for 30s. (4) Fallback: queue for later processing.

findings.title: Critical service without retry logic: paymentMethodService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\paymentMethodService.js handles critical operations without retry logic. Transient failures (network blip, temporary DB lock) cause permanent failure instead of recovering.
findings.suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures: retry 3 times with 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Only retry idempotent operations.

findings.title: Critical service without retry logic: posSettlementIntegrationService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\posSettlementIntegrationService.js handles critical operations without retry logic. Transient failures (network blip, temporary DB lock) cause permanent failure instead of recovering.
findings.suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures: retry 3 times with 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Only retry idempotent operations.

findings.title: No degradation strategy for Email (SMTP) in purchaseOrderService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\purchaseOrderService.js calls Email (SMTP) without timeout, retry limit, or circuit breaker. If Email (SMTP) is slow or down: Notifications silently lost. Without a timeout, requests hang indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool.
findings.suggestion: Add: (1) Timeout: AbortController with 30s limit. (2) Retry with exponential backoff (max 3). (3) Circuit breaker: after 5 failures, skip for 30s. (4) Fallback: queue for later processing.

findings.title: Email sent synchronously without queue in purchaseOrderService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\purchaseOrderService.js sends emails synchronously in the request path. If the SMTP server is slow/down: (1) the API response is delayed, (2) the email is lost on failure. Critical emails (booking confirmations, payment receipts) must be reliable.
findings.suggestion: Queue emails through Bull/Redis queue. Process asynchronously with retry logic. Store email status for monitoring.

findings.title: Critical service without retry logic: roomBookingService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\roomBookingService.js handles critical operations without retry logic. Transient failures (network blip, temporary DB lock) cause permanent failure instead of recovering.
findings.suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures: retry 3 times with 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Only retry idempotent operations.

findings.title: Critical service without retry logic: settlementNotificationService.js
findings.description: backend\src\services\settlementNotificationService.js handles critical operations without retry logic. Transient failures (network blip, temporary DB lock) cause permanent failure instead of recovering.
findings.suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures: retry 3 times with 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Only retry idempotent operations.

findings.title: Health check doesn't verify all dependencies: missing redis, stripe, email
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\healthController.js health check doesn't verify: redis, stripe, email. An "OK" health check while Redis is down gives false confidence. Load balancers route traffic to unhealthy instances.
findings.suggestion: Health check should verify ALL dependencies: DB connection, Redis ping, Stripe reachability. Return detailed status: { db: "ok", redis: "ok", stripe: "degraded" }.

findings.title: 2 critical events may lack notifications
findings.description:
The following events may not trigger proper notifications:
  - payment received (needs email)
  - staff task assigned

Missing notifications mean staff miss critical events and guests don't get confirmations. This leads to operational chaos and poor guest satisfaction.
findings.suggestion: Add email + in-app notifications for all critical business events. Use the existing notification service.

findings.title: Role-based access: 1 expected roles not found in route guards
findings.description: Roles not referenced in route authorization: housekeeping. Without role-specific route guards, these roles either have no dedicated access or share access with other roles.
findings.suggestion: Define specific route access for each role. Housekeeping staff shouldn't access financial data. Guests shouldn't access admin panels.

findings.title: Incomplete CRUD for "guest": missing DELETE
findings.description: The "guest" entity may be missing API operations: DELETE. Incomplete CRUD forces workarounds — staff may need to directly edit the database.
findings.suggestion: Add the missing endpoints for "guest". Every core entity should have: list, get-by-id, create, update, soft-delete.

fixPlan.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
fixPlan.description:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

fixPlan.title: Read-modify-write race condition in critical operation
fixPlan.description: Use MongoDB atomic operators ($inc, $set) or wrap in a session/transaction with optimistic concurrency control (version field).
